name: Wedding Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract or lemon juice
recipeInstructions:
For the cake, preheat oven to 350°F. Spray two 8-inch or 9-inch round cake layer pans with nonstick spray.
Cream together corn oil margarine and fructose until fluffy. Add the 2 whole eggs and mix well. Stir in vanilla extract.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt (if using). Alternately add flour mixture and milk to the butter mixture, mixing well between each addition.
Beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff but not dry. Fold the beaten egg whites into the batter and pour into prepared cake pans.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until lightly golden and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Do not overbake as the cake may become dry.
Allow cakes to cool on wire racks for about 10 minutes before turning them out of the pans and continuing to cool completely.
For the frosting, beat Neufchatel cheese and nonfat milk together until smooth. Gradually add Equal sweetener, stirring after each packet is incorporated. Mix in vanilla extract or lemon juice until well combined.
If desired, add a few drops of food coloring for color. Refrigerate the frosting until firm enough to spread.
Once the cakes have cooled completely, frost between layers and over the top of the cake.
NUTRITION for frosted cake: One serving (1/16 recipe) = 1 1/2 Starch/Bread Exchange, 3 Fat Exchanges; 243 calories; 24 grams carbohydrates; 6 grams protein; 13 grams fat.
